title: briza maxima var. monspessulana
intro: Briza maxima var. monspessulana, also known as Quaking Grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The Quaking Grass can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory and digestive problems.
